content: Decomposition of hydrogen peroxide can be prevented by adding
explanation:
$$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$$ decompose on exposure to light

$$2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{O}_2$$

Thus, its decomposition can be prevented by adding urea as a stabiliser.

$$\mathrm{CO}\left(\mathrm{NH}_2\right)_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow$$ $$\mathrm{\mathop {CO{{(N{H_2})}_2}\,.\,{H_2}{O_2}}\limits_{Urea\,peroxide}}$$

content: Which of the following statement is incorrect for H$$_2$$O$$_2$$ structure?

options.content: It has an open book structure.
It O$$-$$O bond length is 145.8 and O$$-$$H  is 98.8

explanation: H$$_2$$O$$_2$$ has open book structure with O$$-$$O spins. Its dihedral angle is 111$$^\circ$$.
